dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9096 IH: Deport the Terrorists Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Huizenga introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend the Immigration and Nationality Act to revoke the citizenship of any naturalized United States citizen convicted of a terrorism-related crime.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Deport the Terrorists Act of 2026.
Section 340(e) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1451(e)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking When a person shall be convicted and inserting the following:
When a person shall be convicted
by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: When a person shall be convicted of a terrorism-related offense, the court in which such conviction is had shall thereupon revoke, set aside, and declare void the final order admitting such person to citizenship, and shall declare the certificate of naturalization of such person to be canceled. Jurisdiction is hereby conferred on the courts having jurisdiction of the trial of such offence to make such adjudication.
For purposes of this paragraph, the term terrorism-related offense means—

clause.text: an offense under section 2332a of title 18, United States Code;
an offense under section 2332b of title 18, United States Code;
an offense under section 2332f of title 18, United States Code;
an offense under section 2339 of title 18, United States Code;
an offense under section 2339A of title 18, United States Code;
an offense under section 2339B of title 18, United States Code;
an offense under section 2339C of title 18, United States Code;
an offense under section 2339D of title 18, United States Code; or
an offense that is an attempt or conspiracy to commit an offense described in this subparagraph.

section.text: Section 237(a)(4)(B) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1227(a)(4)(B)) is amended by adding at the end the following: Any alien who is convicted of a terrorism-related offense (as such term is defined in section 340(e)(2)(B)) is deportable..
The Secretary of Homeland Security shall prioritize the removal of any person denaturalized under section 340(e)(2) of the Immigration and Nationality Act.
